mappings.rationale: Directly covers political theory including the state, sovereignty, legitimacy, and political action.
Directly covers philosophical foundations of the state, individual rights, sovereignty, and political ideologies.
Covers popular sovereignty, consent of the governed, and constitutional government (subsections A.4.a and A.5).
Directly covers the importance of ideology to political systems and current political ideologies (subsection C.3).
Directly covers nationalism as a major modern political ideology (subsection C.3.b.vii).
Directly covers liberalism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.v).
Directly covers socialism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.viii).
Directly covers communism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.ii).
Directly covers fascism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.iv).
Directly covers anarchism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.i).
Covers populism as a contemporary political tendency and challenge to established political forms.
Covers authoritarian government as a political system contrasted with constitutional governance.
Directly covers Marxism as a major political ideology (subsection C.3.b.vi).
Covers foundational political theory about the exercise of power and statecraft.
Covers the social contract theory and sovereignty as bases of political authority (subsections A.4.a and A.4.b).
Covers theories of popular sovereignty, consent of the governed, and constitutional government.
Covers the social contract, popular sovereignty, and the relationship between the state and the individual.
Covers theories of democratic governance, political action, and the role of public opinion (subsection B.4).
Covers liberal political theory including individual rights, free speech, and representative government.
Directly covers the concept of human rights in political theory (subsection C.2).
Covers freedom of expression as a fundamental political right and the influence of public opinion (subsection B.4).
Covers the relationship between the state and the individual including rights, duties, and political participation.
Directly covers the historical development of political theories from ancient to modern times.
Covers neoliberal political ideology and its relationship to the state and market.
Covers the problem of religion and politics, relevant to the church-state issue (subsection C.4.a).
Covers the separation of church and state as a political principle (subsection C.4.a).
Covers terrorism as a form of political action by special-interest and revolutionary groups.
Covers revolutionary political action as a method of changing political forms.
Covers utilitarian political philosophy as a basis for government policy and individual rights.
Covers utopian political thought and visions of ideal states and political organization.
Covers progressive political ideology as a reform movement within democratic systems.
Covers feminist political theory and its challenges to traditional political structures.
Covers Arendt's political theory including the nature of political power, authority, and totalitarianism.
Covers the issue of centralization vs. decentralization of political power (subsection C.4.d).
Covers how political authority is structured and exercised in contemporary political systems.
Covers the origins of constitutional government and limitations on state power (subsection A.5.a).
Covers political tolerance as a foundational principle of liberal democratic governance and church-state relations.
Covers the rule of law as a feature of constitutional government and a check on political power (subsection A.5.b).
